extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the Nancy sprint by following yellow jersey ALAPHILIPPE Julian's leadout wheel in final 800m after Caleb EWAN got boxed.
tactical_patterns: Yellow jersey ALAPHILIPPE Julian led out teammate VIVIANI Elia in final 800m, demonstrating Quick-Step's dual-threat strategy.
EWAN Caleb boxed inside barriers late after being positioned perfectly third wheel, showing positioning matters more than power.
INEOS moved entire team forward on uncategorized climb approaching finale for safety, revealing GC-first mentality even on sprint stages.
BORA's VAN RENSBURG Reinardt pulled final 5km for SAGAN Peter, but leadout isolated compared to Quick-Step's yellow jersey advantage.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: used yellow jersey position to leadout teammate VIVIANI Elia from 800m, proving race leader status enables sprint train dominance
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: trapped inside barriers despite perfect third-wheel position earlier, suggesting leadout coordination failure or positioning inexperience
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: first Tour stage win since 2014 by exploiting ALAPHILIPPE Julian's yellow jersey pulling power
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: positioned behind EWAN Caleb but Quick-Step's yellow jersey leadout strategy neutralized BORA's numerical advantage
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: INEOS prioritized moving him forward on uncategorized climb 10km out, indicating crash-avoidance over sprint support
